CLINICAL TRIAL DATA BUNDLE: PHASE IIb STUDY OF XR-7491
PROTOCOL STATUS HEADER
PROTOCOL ID: AURORA-2
SPONSOR: PharmaCorp Research
STUDY DRUG: XR-7491
INDICATION: Rheumatoid Arthritis (Moderate-to-Severe)
DRUG CLASS: JAK1/3 Inhibitor
PHASE: IIb
CURRENT STATUS: Active
PROTOCOL VERSION: 3.0
DATE OF REPORT: 2025-03-03
SITE NUMBER: SITE-001
INSTITUTION: North American Clinical Research Center
PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR: PI_Smith, MD, FACP
SITE DIRECTOR: J. Doe, RN, CCRP
CLINICAL RESEARCH ASSOCIATE: M. Chen, BS
MONITORING VISIT TYPE: Routine Unannounced Monitoring / Data Review
REGULATORY COMPLIANCE STATUS: Compliant
GCP STATUS: Good Clinical Practice Compliant
INSTITUTIONAL REVIEW BOARD (IRB): North American IRB, Protocol #99821
ELECTRONIC DATA CAPTURE (EDC) SYSTEM: Oracle Clinical v12.4
DATABASE LOCK STATUS: Open for Query Resolution
DATA QUALITY AUDIT STATUS: Pending Final Review

STUDY OBJECTIVE: Evaluate efficacy and safety of XR-7491 in moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis.
PRIMARY ENDPOINT: ACR20 response rate at Week 12.
SECONDARY ENDPOINTS: ACR50, ACR70, DAS28-CRP, HAQ-DI, remission rates.
STUDY DESIGN: Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled, Parallel-Group, Multicenter.
TOTAL PLANNED ENROLLMENT: 240 subjects.
RANDOMIZATION RATIO: 3:3:3:1 (5mg, 15mg, 30mg BID, Placebo).
NUMBER OF DOSING ARMS: 4
ARM 1: XR-7491 5mg Oral Tablet, BID.
ARM 2: XR-7491 15mg Oral Tablet, BID.
ARM 3: XR-7491 30mg Oral Tablet, BID.
ARM 4: Matching Placebo Oral Tablet, BID.
STUDY SITES: 12 clinical centers across North America.
SITE-001 STATUS: Open and Active.
INCLUSION CRITERIA:
1. Male or female, age 18 to 75 years.
2. Diagnosis of Rheumatoid Arthritis meeting 2010 ACR/EULAR Classification Criteria.
3. Disease duration >6 months.
4. Active disease defined as ≥6 swollen joints and ≥6 tender joints at screening.
5. Inadequate response to at least one Disease-Modifying Antirheumatic Drug (DMARD).
6. Stable dose of background methotrexate for ≥8 weeks prior to screening.
7. Signed Informed Consent Form (ICF).
EXCLUSION CRITERIA:
1. Active hepatitis B or C infection.
2. ALT >1.5x Upper Limit of Normal (ULN).
3. AST >2x ULN.
4. History of malignancy within 5 years (excluding non-melanoma skin cancer).
5. Active tuberculosis or history of latent TB without treatment.
6. Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
7. Concomitant use of biologics or JAK inhibitors within 4 weeks.
BASELINE VISIT SCHEDULE: Scheduled for all randomized subjects within 24 hours of randomization.
BASELINE ASSESSMENTS: Physical exam, vital signs, ECG, laboratory panel (CBC, CMP, LFTs, lipids), pregnancy test, RA disease activity scores (DAS28, ACR core set), concomitant medication review.
STUDY DURATION: 12 weeks treatment, 4 weeks follow-up.
TOTAL STUDY DURATION: 16 weeks.
SCHEDULE OF EVENTS:
Visit 1 (Screening): Informed consent, eligibility check, ICF signature, baseline labs.
Visit 2 (Baseline/Day 1): Randomization, drug dispensing, first dose administration, safety assessment.
Visit 3 (Week 2): Safety, tolerability, compliance check.
Visit 4 (Week 4): Efficacy assessment, labs, safety.
Visit 5 (Week 6): Efficacy assessment, labs, safety.
Visit 6 (Week 8): Efficacy assessment, labs, safety.
Visit 7 (Week 12): Primary endpoint assessment, labs, safety, final dosing.
Visit 8 (Week 16): Follow-up, safety, discontinuation.

RANDOMIZATION PERFORMED: Yes.
RANDOMIZATION CODE: ARM-04 (Placebo).
RANDOMIZATION METHOD: Interactive Response Technology (IRT) System, Version 5.2.

BASELINE LABS DRAWN: Yes.

LAB SPECIMENS: Serum, Plasma, Urine.
SPECIMEN HANDLING: Centrifuged at 3000 RPM for 10 minutes. Aliquoted into cryovials. Stored at -80°C pending shipment.
BASELINE LAB RESULTS: Within normal limits.
NO ADVERSE EVENTS REPORTED AT SCREENING: Confirmed.
SCREENING FAILURES (N=6):
Subject S-001: ALT 1.8x ULN (Exclusion criteria).
Subject S-002: Positive Hepatitis C antibody.
Subject S-003: Inadequate response to methotrexate (failed criteria).
Subject S-004: Pregnancy test positive.
Subject S-005: Uncontrolled hypertension (BP >160/100).
Subject S-006: Withdrawn consent prior to randomization.

CLINICAL TRIAL DATA BUNDLE: PHASE IIb STUDY OF XR-7491
STUDY TITLE: RANDOMIZED, DOUBLE-BLIND, PLACEBO-CONTROLLED STUDY OF XR-7491 IN SUBJECTS WITH ACTIVE RHEUMATOID ARTHRITIS
PROTOCOL VERSION: 2.0
DATE OF REPORT: 2025-03-09
REPORT TYPE: MONITORING SNAPSHOT / DATA BUNDLE
GENERATION ID: BUNDLE-20250309-001

================================================================================
SECTION 1: PROTOCOL STATUS HEADER AND SITE COORDINATION
================================================================================

STUDY OVERVIEW
The Phase IIb clinical trial for XR-7491, an oral Janus Kinase (JAK) inhibitor, is currently in the baseline data collection and initial enrollment phase. The study is designed to evaluate the efficacy and safety of three distinct dosing levels (5mg, 15mg, and 30mg) of XR-7491 administered twice daily (BID) compared to placebo in adult subjects with moderate to severe rheumatoid arthritis (RA) who have had an inadequate response to at least one Disease-Modifying Antirheumatic Drug (DMARD).

CURRENT ENROLLMENT STATUS

Total Subjects Enrolled: 35
Total Subjects Randomized: 35
Subjects Completed Baseline: 35
Subjects Completed Visit 1: 35
Subjects with Completed Dosing: 32
Subjects with Pending Dosing: 3

SITE COORDINATION COMMUNICATION LOG
Date of Communication: 2025-03-09
Communication Type: Electronic Mail Distribution
Email ID: EMAIL-001
Sender: Clinical Operations (Clinical Ops)
Recipient List: Principal Investigators (Site PIs) across all 24 active sites.

MESSAGE CONTENT AND SUMMARY:
The Clinical Operations team issued a formal protocol update notification to all Site PIs. This communication served as a mandatory distribution of revised protocol documents and specific guidance regarding recent amendments to the study design. The primary objective of this communication was to ensure uniformity in the application of inclusion criteria and to reinforce adherence to strict visit window compliance protocols.

Key points addressed in the correspondence included:
1. Protocol Amendments: A summary of the specific changes made to the protocol version 2.0 was included. These amendments were necessitated by feedback from the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) and the need to refine the safety monitoring parameters for the JAK inhibitor class.
2. Inclusion Criteria Updates: A specific reminder was issued regarding the updated inclusion criteria. The Clinical Ops team explicitly stated: "Reminder: Inclusion criteria updates." This directive emphasized that all new screening attempts must strictly adhere to the revised criteria regarding prior DMARD exposure and disease activity scores (DAS28).
3. Visit Window Compliance: The communication reinforced the critical nature of maintaining the designated visit windows. The specific instruction provided was: "Visit window compliance required." This directive clarified that deviations beyond the acceptable tolerance could result in the exclusion of data from the primary efficacy analysis.
4. Visit Window Specifications: The standard visit window was defined as +/- 3 days from the scheduled date. Any visit occurring outside this window requires a deviation report and justification.
5. New Laboratory Requirements: The notification detailed that new laboratory requirements have been added to the Case Report Forms (CRFs). These additions include specific biomarkers for monitoring JAK inhibitor safety profiles.
6. Safety Reporting Timeline: The safety reporting timeline for Adverse Events (AEs) and Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) has been updated. The new standard requires reporting of AEs within 24 hours of discovery.
7. Training Materials: All updated training materials, including the revised Good Clinical Practice (GCP) modules specific to XR-7491, have been uploaded to the central site management portal.
8. Query Resolution Rate: The current status of data queries across the 24 active sites was reported. The query resolution rate is currently at 95%. This metric indicates a high level of data quality and site responsiveness.
9. Site Inquiries: The communication also noted that site inquiries regarding the protocol amendments have been addressed. A dedicated help desk channel was established to handle follow-up questions from the PIs.

SECTION 7: DATA SAFETY MONITORING BOARD (DSMB) NOTES
================================================================================

DSMB MEETING MINUTES
Date: 2025-03-09
Meeting Type: Ad Hoc Review
Study: Phase IIb XR-7491
Protocol Version: 2.0
Chairperson: Dr. Robert Chen
Secretary: Dr. Emily White

AGENDA
1. Review of Safety Data (AEs, SAEs, Lab Abnormalities).
2. Review of Efficacy Data (Preliminary DAS28 scores).
3. Review of Enrollment Status.
4. Discussion of Protocol Amendments.
5. Recommendations for Continued Study Conduct.

SAFETY REVIEW
The DSMB reviewed the safety data accumulated since the last meeting.
- Total Adverse Events Reported: 1 (AE-003).
- Severity: Grade 1 (Mild).
- Serious Adverse Events: 0.
- Deaths: 0.
- Withdrawals due to AEs: 0.
- Laboratory Abnormalities: No clinically significant abnormalities identified.
- ALT/AST Levels: Within normal limits for all subjects.
- Hematology: Within normal limits for all subjects.

The DSMB noted that the adverse event reported (Fatigue, GI Disturbance) is an expected class effect for JAK inhibitors. The event was mild and did not require intervention. The relationship to the study drug was assessed as "Possible." No new safety signals were identified.

EFFICACY REVIEW
Preliminary efficacy data was reviewed.
- DAS28 Scores: Baseline scores are consistent with the inclusion criteria (DAS28 > 5.1).
- Early Response: No early response data available yet (Study in baseline phase).
- Treatment Arms: All arms (Placebo, 5mg, 15mg, 30mg) represented.
- Data Quality: High.

The DSMB noted that the study is in the baseline phase. No efficacy conclusions can be drawn at this time. The data quality is sufficient to support future efficacy analysis.

ENROLLMENT REVIEW
The DSMB reviewed the enrollment status.
- Total Sites: 24.
- Sites Active: 24.
- Total Subjects Enrolled: 35.
- Enrollment Rate: On track.
- Protocol Amendments: The updates to inclusion criteria and visit windows have been communicated to all sites.

The DSMB noted that the enrollment rate is satisfactory. The protocol amendments have been implemented. The query resolution rate of 95% is acceptable.

PROTOCOL AMENDMENT REVIEW
The DSMB reviewed the protocol amendments distributed via EMAIL-001.
- Inclusion Criteria Updates: The amendments are appropriate to ensure subject safety and data quality.
- Visit Window Compliance: The +/- 3 day window is standard and feasible.
- New Lab Requirements: The new lab requirements are necessary for safety monitoring.
- Safety Reporting Timeline: The 24-hour timeline is appropriate.

The DSMB approved the protocol amendments. The amendments are in effect for all sites.

RECOMMENDATIONS
The DSMB recommended the following:
1. Continue the study as planned.
2. Maintain strict adherence to the updated visit window compliance.
3. Continue close monitoring of adverse events, particularly fatigue and GI disturbances.
4. Ensure all sites adhere to the new safety reporting timeline.
5. Maintain blinding integrity.

CONCLUSIONS
The DSMB concluded that the study is proceeding safely and according to the protocol. The safety profile is consistent with the known profile of JAK inhibitors. The enrollment is on track. The data quality is high. No changes to the study design are recommended at this time.

- Report Generated: 2025-03-15
- Reporting Completed Within 24 Hours: Yes.

CLINICAL DESCRIPTION:
The subject reported a mild headache. The intensity was graded as 1 on the NCI-CTCAE scale. The subject described the pain as a dull pressure in the frontal region. The headache resolved spontaneously after 4 hours. The subject was advised to rest and use acetaminophen as needed for pain relief. No further medical intervention was required.

EXPECTED CLASS EFFECT:
- Expected class effect noted.
- Literature review indicates that headache is a common, mild adverse event associated with JAK inhibitors.
- The frequency of this event in the current study is within the expected range based on Phase I data.

SAFETY MONITORING:
The event was recorded in the Electronic Data Capture (EDC) system. The Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) will review this event during the next scheduled meeting. No changes to the study protocol or monitoring plan are required at this time. The subject continues to be monitored for any recurrence of symptoms or development of new adverse events.

CONCOMITANT MEDICATION REVIEW:
The subject was taking Acetaminophen 500mg for the headache. This medication is allowed under the protocol as a rescue medication for mild pain. The dose was within the recommended limits. No interactions with the study drug (XR-7491) were identified.

1. PROTOCOL STATUS HEADER AND ADMINISTRATIVE OVERVIEW

1.1 Study Identification and Governance
The clinical trial data bundle for the Phase IIb study of XR-7491, designated internally as red_herring for the monitoring period ending 2025-03-27, is hereby submitted for review. This document constitutes a routine snapshot of the monitoring data associated with the investigational product XR-7491. The study is conducted in accordance with the Declaration of Helsinki, ICH-GCP guidelines, and local regulatory requirements. The protocol version currently active for all sites is Protocol Version 2.0. This version was approved by the Institutional Review Board (IRB) and the Independent Ethics Committee (IEC) on 2024-11-15. The study sponsor, XR Pharma Inc., retains full responsibility for the conduct of the trial and the integrity of the data.

1.2 Site Information and Principal Investigator
The primary data submission originates from Site-009. The site is located in a tertiary care center specializing in rheumatology. The Principal Investigator for Site-009 is PI_Taylor. PI_Taylor holds a current license to practice medicine and has completed the required Good Clinical Practice (GCP) training modules. The site activation was approved by the sponsor on 2025-01-10. The site has been operational for 12 weeks as of the data cutoff date. The site contact information is on file in the Investigator Site File (ISF) under document code SITE-009-CON. The site manager is responsible for daily operations and staff supervision. Staff turnover is within acceptable limits. All clinical staff have signed delegation of authority logs. The site maintains a secure facility for the storage of investigational products and source documents. The facility meets the requirements for controlled substance storage where applicable.

1.3 Enrollment Status and Randomization
The enrollment status for the reporting period is Open. The site is actively recruiting subjects meeting the inclusion and exclusion criteria. The total subjects enrolled across all sites is 78. At Site-009, randomization has been completed for 75 subjects. The randomization code for the most recent subjects is ARM-04 (Placebo). Randomization was performed using an Interactive Web Response System (IWRS). The IWRS is managed by the sponsor's data management team. The randomization schedule is stratified by disease duration and prior biologic use. The allocation ratio is 1:1:1:1 across the four treatment arms. The treatment arms include Placebo, 5mg XR-7491, 15mg XR-7491, and 30mg XR-7491. Blinding is maintained throughout the study period. Unblinding procedures are available only for medical emergencies. The study is double-blind. Neither the investigator nor the subject knows the treatment assignment. The code break envelope is sealed and stored in the ISF.

1.4 Protocol Version and Amendments
Protocol Version 2.0 is active. This version superseded Version 1.0 which was used during the pilot phase. The amendment was implemented to adjust the dosing schedule. The amendment was submitted to the regulatory authorities on 2025-02-01. The implementation date was 2025-02-15. All subjects enrolled prior to the amendment are grandfathered under the original protocol provisions unless they meet the new criteria. The new criteria apply to subjects enrolled on or after 2025-02-15. This ensures consistency in the safety profile analysis. The sponsor has notified all sites of the change. Site-009 has updated the subject consent forms to reflect the new protocol version. The informed consent obtained for each subject references the current version of the protocol. The consent form includes a description of the investigational product and the potential risks.

2. ADVERSE EVENT REPORTS

2.1 Adverse Event Collection Methodology
Adverse events are collected from the date of informed consent until the end of the study or 30 days after the last dose of investigational product. The collection method involves daily monitoring by the study nurse and weekly review by the Principal Investigator. All adverse events are recorded in the Case Report Forms (CRFs). The MedDRA coding dictionary version 28.0 is used for adverse event coding. The coding is performed by the sponsor's medical writing team. The coding is verified by a medical monitor. Adverse events are categorized by system organ class and preferred term. The severity is graded according to the National Cancer Institute Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events (CTCAE) version 5.0. The relationship to the investigational product is assessed using the Naranjo Scale or sponsor-specific criteria.

2.2 Adverse Event Summary Table
The following table summarizes the adverse events reported during the monitoring period. The data cutoff for this section is 2025-03-26. The total number of subjects enrolled is 78. The total number of adverse events recorded is 16 minor. There are no serious adverse events reported. There are no adverse events of special interest reported. The adverse events are distributed across the treatment arms. The distribution is consistent with the known safety profile of JAK inhibitors. The events are non-specific. The events do not show a dose-response relationship. The events resolved without intervention in 14 cases. Two cases required medical intervention. The medical intervention did not result in discontinuation. The safety signal assessment is ongoing. The DSMB Review 6 notes no safety signals. This assessment is based on the aggregate data. The data review conducted by the DSMB was comprehensive. The review included all raw data and source documents. The Safety Monitor reviewed the data independently. The Safety Monitor is a qualified medical professional. The Efficacy Committee reviewed the data for consistency.

4. EFFICACY REPORT

4.1 Efficacy Metrics and Definitions
The efficacy metrics for the study are defined in the protocol. The primary endpoint is the proportion of subjects achieving an ACR20 response at Week 12. The secondary endpoints include ACR50, ACR70, DAS28 score reduction, and CDAI score reduction. The ACR20 response is defined as a 20% improvement in tender and swollen joint counts and 20% improvement in at least three of the five remaining criteria. The ACR50 response is defined as a 50% improvement in tender and swollen joint counts and 50% improvement in at least three of the five remaining criteria. The ACR70 response is defined as a 70% improvement in tender and swollen joint counts and 70% improvement in at least three of the five remaining criteria. The DAS28 score reduction is calculated using the tender joint count, swollen joint count, patient global assessment, and C-reactive protein. The CDAI score reduction is calculated using the tender joint count, swollen joint count, patient global assessment, and physician global assessment. STUDY OVERVIEW
The present document constitutes a clinical trial data bundle for the Phase IIb escalation study of XR-7491. XR-7491 is an oral JAK inhibitor evaluated for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis. The study utilizes a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled design. The current data snapshot reflects the status as of 2025-04-08. The study is currently in the escalation phase. Protocol version 2.0 is active for all participating centers.

================================================================================

SITE IDENTIFICATION: SITE-014
PRINCIPAL INVESTIGATOR: PI_Harris
ENROLLMENT STATUS: Open
STUDY PHASE: Escalation
CURRENT COHORT: Cohort 4 (30mg Dose Level)
LAST DATA CUT-OFF: 2025-04-10
NEXT SCHEDULED REVIEW: Week 12

ENROLLMENT LOG DETAILS:
The enrollment log for SITE-014, recorded on 2025-04-11, indicates active recruitment and baseline assessment procedures are ongoing. The site activation was approved in accordance with regulatory requirements and internal SOPs. The protocol version 2.0 is currently active and in use for all subject interactions at this location.

Subject_028 was enrolled on 2025-04-11. Subject_028 is a female, 53 years of age. Subject_029 was also enrolled on 2025-04-11. Subject_029 is a male, 58 years of age. Baseline labs were drawn for both subjects on the date of enrollment. Informed consent was obtained for both subjects in compliance with Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines. Randomization was completed for 110 subjects across the study population prior to the current reporting window.

Subject_028 was assigned to the 30mg dose level. The randomization code for Subject_028 is ARM-03 (30mg). Subject_029 was assigned to the 30mg dose level. The randomization code for Subject_029 is ARM-03 (30mg). Drug administration was performed for both subjects immediately following the baseline assessment and randomization process. The Investigational Product (IP) was dispensed in accordance with the dosing schedule defined in the protocol.

Randomization code: ARM-03 (30mg).
Informed consent obtained.

The total number of subjects enrolled in the study as of the data cutoff date of 2025-04-10 is 115. This figure includes all subjects enrolled across all participating sites. The enrollment status remains Open. No site closures or suspensions have been reported for the current reporting period. The enrollment trajectory is consistent with the projected timeline outlined in the master protocol.

SITE-014 is currently operating under standard operating procedures for subject management. PI_Harris has oversight of all clinical activities at the site. The site staff has completed all required training modules relevant to the current protocol version. The site is maintaining a current master file and essential documents are up to date.

Enrollment details for the current cohort:
Cohort 1 (Placebo): 28 subjects enrolled.
Cohort 2 (5mg): 29 subjects enrolled.
Cohort 3 (15mg): 30 subjects enrolled.
Cohort 4 (30mg): 28 subjects enrolled.
Total enrolled: 115 subjects.

Randomization allocation ratio: 1:1:1:1.
Blinding maintenance procedures are in place.
Blinding maintained.

The site has reported no significant deviations from the protocol regarding enrollment procedures. All consent forms are signed and dated. The subject identification codes are stored securely in the electronic data capture (EDC) system. The chain of custody for biological samples is documented.

Baseline assessment protocols were followed for Subject_028 and Subject_029. Vital signs were recorded. Physical examinations were conducted. Medical history was updated. Concomitant medications were documented. The baseline disease activity scores were recorded for both subjects.

The enrollment log for 2025-04-11 reflects routine administrative processing. No delays in subject screening or enrollment were noted. The site capacity remains sufficient to handle the current enrollment rate. The site is in good standing with the sponsor.

SECTION 1: PROTOCOL STATUS HEADER AND STUDY OVERVIEW

This document serves as a comprehensive monitoring snapshot for the XR-7491 Phase IIb clinical trial. The study utilizes an oral JAK inhibitor designated as XR-7491 for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis. The current phase of the study is designated as escalation. The data contained within this bundle reflects the status of the trial as of the date of issue, 2025-04-17. All data entries correspond to the active protocol version 2.0. This version of the protocol was active at the time of data collection and reporting.

The study involves multiple sites. The primary focus of this snapshot is the data generated from Site-016. This site has been approved for activation. Site activation approved status is recorded in the central registry. The Principal Investigator for this site is PI_Thompson. The PI is responsible for the oversight of all clinical activities at this location. The site ID is recorded as SITE-016 in the master site list. The enrollment status for this site is currently Open. Open status indicates that subject recruitment is active and ongoing.

The study design includes a double-blind, placebo-controlled structure. Blinding maintained status is confirmed for all arms. The blinding code is held centrally by the Sponsor. The Sponsor Rep is the designated contact for data queries and protocol deviations. The DSMB Chair oversees the safety review process. The Safety Monitor is responsible for the daily oversight of adverse event reporting. The Efficacy Committee is responsible for the interim analysis of efficacy metrics.

The total number of subjects enrolled across all sites at the time of this report is 130. Total subjects enrolled: 130. This figure includes subjects from Site-016 and all other participating centers. The escalation phase involves dose titration to determine the optimal therapeutic window. The dose levels under investigation include 5mg, 15mg, and 30mg. A placebo arm is also included in the randomization scheme. The randomization code is ARM-03 for the 30mg arm. The randomization code is ARM-01 for the placebo arm. The randomization code is ARM-02 for the 5mg arm. The randomization code is ARM-04 for the 15mg arm.

The protocol version 2.0 active status is noted in the header. This version supersedes the previous version 1.0. Any amendments to the protocol are tracked in the amendment log. The amendment log is reviewed by the DSMB. The DSMB notes are documented in the meeting minutes. The meeting ID for the most recent review is DSMB-013. The meeting date is 2025-04-17. The attendees for this meeting include the DSMB Chair and the Sponsor Rep.

The data cutoff for this report is 2025-04-16. Data cutoff: 2025-04-16. All data collected prior to this date is included in the analysis. Data collected after this date is reserved for the next reporting cycle. The next review is scheduled for Week 14. Next review scheduled: Week 14. This schedule ensures regular monitoring of safety and efficacy signals. The monitoring frequency is consistent with the Phase IIb escalation requirements.

The study follows Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines. All personnel have completed required training. Site staff training up to date status is recorded in the training log. 1. PROTOCOL STATUS HEADER AND ADMINISTRATIVE OVERVIEW

This document serves as the consolidated monitoring snapshot for the XR-7491 Phase IIb study. The data contained herein reflects the status of the study as of 2025-04-20. All procedures described herein were conducted in accordance with the Good Clinical Practice guidelines as defined by the International Conference on Harmonisation and the Declaration of Helsinki. The study protocol, version 2.0, is active and governs all activities at the participating sites. The primary objective of this study is to evaluate the efficacy of XR-7491, an oral JAK inhibitor, compared to placebo in reducing the signs and symptoms of rheumatoid arthritis. Secondary objectives include the evaluation of safety and tolerability of XR-7491.

The study sites are categorized by geographic region and enrollment capacity. This specific bundle focuses on data received from SITE-017 and SITE-001, along with central laboratory data and the Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) review. The Principal Investigator for SITE-017 is PI_Garcia. The Principal Investigator for SITE-001 is PI_Johnson. Both sites are currently open for enrollment. The site activation approval was granted on 2024-11-15. The randomization system is managed centrally by the Interactive Web Response System (IWRS). The IWRS ensures that treatment allocation is concealed from the investigator and study staff until the point of assignment.

Regulatory compliance is monitored through regular audits and inspections. The current regulatory status for SITE-017 is compliant. The current regulatory status for SITE-001 is compliant. All deviations from the protocol are documented in the Deviation Log. There are no major protocol deviations recorded for the period 2025-04-01 to 2025-04-20. The informed consent process is verified through the signed copies stored in the Investigator Site File. The informed consent form used is the current version approved by the Institutional Review Board (IRB). The IRB for SITE-017 is IRB_CENTRAL_WEST. The IRB for SITE-001 is IRB_CENTRAL_EAST.

The study drug, XR-7491, is manufactured under Good Manufacturing Practice conditions. The investigational product account is maintained at each site. The inventory of XR-7491 is tracked by lot number and expiration date. The chain of custody for the investigational product is maintained in the Investigational Product Log. The medication counts are reconciled monthly. The reconciliation process ensures that the number of tablets dispensed matches the number of tablets returned plus the number of tablets consumed. The dispensing log is reviewed by the Compliance Officer at the end of each month. The Compliance Officer is responsible for verifying the accuracy of the medication logs.

The study duration for each subject is approximately 52 weeks. The follow-up period is 8 weeks post-treatment. The primary endpoint is the proportion of subjects achieving ACR20 response at Week 12. The secondary endpoints include DAS28 score reduction and ACR50 response. The statistical analysis plan defines the methods for handling missing data. The imputation method used is Last Observation Carried Forward. The analysis population includes all randomized subjects who received at least one dose of study medication. The safety population includes all subjects who received at least one dose of study medication. The efficacy population includes all subjects who have at least one post-baseline efficacy assessment.

The Data Safety Monitoring Board (DSMB) meets at scheduled intervals to review unblinded safety and efficacy data. The DSMB composition includes independent experts in rheumatology, biostatistics, and pharmacology. The DSMB provides recommendations to the Sponsor regarding the continuation of the study. The DSMB reviews are confidential and the data is presented in an aggregated format. The DSMB notes are recorded in the meeting minutes and stored in the secure document management system. The DSMB Chair is Dr. Smith. The DSMB Vice-Chair is Dr. Jones. The Sponsor Representative is Dr. Williams.

STUDY OVERVIEW AND BACKGROUND

This clinical trial data bundle represents a routine snapshot of the Phase IIb study of XR-7491, an oral JAK inhibitor indicated for the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis. The study design is a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter trial. The primary objective is to evaluate the efficacy and safety of XR-7491 at multiple dosage levels compared to placebo. The study is currently in the active enrollment and treatment phase. All procedures are conducted in accordance with the Declaration of Helsinki and Good Clinical Practice (GCP) guidelines. The protocol version 2.0 is active for all participating sites. Site activation approved for SITE-018 and SITE-001.

The study population consists of adult patients diagnosed with moderate to severe rheumatoid arthritis who have failed to respond to at least one disease-modifying antirheumatic drug (DMARD). The study involves stratification by disease activity score and prior treatment history. Randomization is stratified by center to ensure balance across study arms. The randomization code is managed by an interactive response technology (IRT) system. The randomization code: ARM-03 (30mg) is assigned to specific subjects based on the IRT algorithm.

questions.prompt: Are there any concerning trends in the safety data across dose arms?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: The high-dose arm (30mg) is showing subtle ALT elevations — mean ALT has moved from 22 to 31 U/L, still within normal range but trending upward. Two individual subjects have ALT values of 35-38 U/L. This is very early and could be noise, but the dose-dependency is worth monitoring.
key_facts: ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory

questions.prompt: What are the most common adverse events reported across all arms?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: The most common adverse events are headache and GI disturbances (nausea, diarrhea) — expected class effects of JAK inhibitors. These are Grade 1-2 and occur across all active arms.

questions.prompt: Is there a dose-dependent safety signal emerging in the liver function data?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: Yes. The 30mg arm shows a clear upward ALT trajectory: baseline 22 → Week 8 mean 31 → now 42 U/L (above ULN). Four subjects are above ULN, one at 3.1×ULN requiring dose reduction. The 15mg arm is also beginning to trend upward (33→37 U/L). The 5mg and placebo arms are stable. The pattern is dose-dependent.
key_facts: ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory
the hepatotoxicity signal has a 4-6 week lag from dose initiation making early monitoring reviews appear clean
the 15mg mid-dose arm is now showing the same ALT trajectory the 30mg arm showed 4 weeks earlier

questions.prompt: Are the placebo ALT elevations evidence that liver effects are not drug-related?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: No. The placebo ALT elevations have identified causes — pre-existing NAFLD, alcohol use, and statin initiation. These are background noise, not evidence against a drug effect. The dose-dependent pattern in the active arms (30mg > 15mg > 5mg ≈ placebo) is the relevant signal.
key_facts: placebo subjects with elevated baseline ALT from pre-existing conditions created noise that masked the drug signal
ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory

questions.prompt: When was the hepatotoxicity signal first detectable in the data?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: The signal was first detectable around Week 8, when the high-dose arm mean ALT began diverging from other arms. However, individual values were still within normal range, and the DSMB Review 2 concluded "liver function within normal limits." The 4-6 week lag from dose initiation to ALT elevation meant the earliest monitoring windows showed clean data.
key_facts: the hepatotoxicity signal has a 4-6 week lag from dose initiation making early monitoring reviews appear clean
retrospective trajectory analysis revealed the safety signal was present from Week 8 but missed in real-time monitoring
ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory

questions.prompt: Is there sufficient evidence to recommend suspending the 30mg dose arm?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: Yes. Four subjects have ALT above ULN, one required dose reduction at 3.1×ULN, and the trajectory is accelerating. The dose-dependence is clear. The mid-dose arm is lagging by ~4 weeks on the same curve. Continuing the 30mg arm risks Hy's Law cases.
key_facts: ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory
the 15mg mid-dose arm is now showing the same ALT trajectory the 30mg arm showed 4 weeks earlier
the hepatotoxicity signal has a 4-6 week lag from dose initiation making early monitoring reviews appear clean

questions.prompt: What is the complete hepatotoxicity profile of XR-7491 based on all available data?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: XR-7491 causes delayed, dose-dependent hepatocyte injury. The mechanism produces ALT elevations beginning 4-6 weeks after dose initiation. The 30mg arm trajectory: baseline 22 → Week 8 31 → Week 12 42 → Week 16 >3×ULN in multiple subjects. Two subjects met Hy's Law criteria. The 15mg arm shows the same trajectory lagged by ~4 weeks. The signal was present from Week 8 but was masked by the onset lag, placebo noise from pre-existing conditions, and the strong efficacy signal creating confirmation bias.
key_facts: ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory
the hepatotoxicity signal has a 4-6 week lag from dose initiation making early monitoring reviews appear clean
two high-dose subjects met Hy's Law criteria with ALT >3xULN concurrent with bilirubin >2xULN
the 15mg mid-dose arm is now showing the same ALT trajectory the 30mg arm showed 4 weeks earlier
placebo subjects with elevated baseline ALT from pre-existing conditions created noise that masked the drug signal
retrospective trajectory analysis revealed the safety signal was present from Week 8 but missed in real-time monitoring
the highest-dose arm had the best efficacy which created pressure to interpret borderline liver values as acceptable

questions.prompt: If the DSMB had required trajectory analysis rather than point-in-time monitoring, when could the hold have been issued?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: A trajectory analysis comparing dose-arm mean ALT slopes would have detected the divergence at Week 8-10, approximately 6-8 weeks before the actual clinical hold. This would have prevented the Hy's Law cases, which occurred at Weeks 15-16. The point-in-time approach of comparing individual values against ULN missed the signal because values were within normal range until Week 12.
key_facts: the hepatotoxicity signal has a 4-6 week lag from dose initiation making early monitoring reviews appear clean
retrospective trajectory analysis revealed the safety signal was present from Week 8 but missed in real-time monitoring
two high-dose subjects met Hy's Law criteria with ALT >3xULN concurrent with bilirubin >2xULN

questions.prompt: What changes to the safety monitoring protocol should be recommended for future trials of this drug class?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: Implement trajectory-based liver monitoring comparing dose-arm mean ALT slopes, not just individual values against ULN. Require blinded aggregate trajectory plots at each DSMB review. Set dose-arm divergence thresholds (e.g., >10 U/L separation sustained over 2 visits). Account for the 4-6 week onset lag by extending initial monitoring through Week 12. Pre-stratify for baseline liver conditions to separate drug signal from noise. Consider pharmacogenomic screening for hepatotoxicity susceptibility.
key_facts: the hepatotoxicity signal has a 4-6 week lag from dose initiation making early monitoring reviews appear clean
placebo subjects with elevated baseline ALT from pre-existing conditions created noise that masked the drug signal
retrospective trajectory analysis revealed the safety signal was present from Week 8 but missed in real-time monitoring
the highest-dose arm had the best efficacy which created pressure to interpret borderline liver values as acceptable

questions.prompt: Does the strong efficacy data justify continuing the 30mg arm despite the liver signal?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: No. Two subjects have met Hy's Law criteria, which carries a 10-50% risk of fatal drug-induced liver injury. The efficacy benefit does not justify this risk, particularly when lower doses also show efficacy (though less robust). The risk-benefit calculus may support continued development of the 5mg or 15mg doses with enhanced monitoring, but the 30mg arm should remain suspended.
key_facts: two high-dose subjects met Hy's Law criteria with ALT >3xULN concurrent with bilirubin >2xULN
the highest-dose arm had the best efficacy which created pressure to interpret borderline liver values as acceptable
ALT elevations in the XR-7491 trial are dose-dependent with the 30mg arm showing the clearest trajectory
